requirements.task_name: Request a quote for the cheapest 20–30 sqm rear extension under $80,000
requirements.task_description: Find rear extension packages between 20–30 sqm, filter to a maximum budget of $80,000, sort by price, select the cheapest result, and submit a quote request for that package.

requirements.task_name: Book a weekday afternoon consultation for the cheapest kitchen renovation under $35,000
requirements.task_description: Find kitchen renovation packages under $35,000, sort by price to select the cheapest one, and book an on-site consultation on a weekday afternoon time slot for that package.

requirements.task_name: Choose the larger floor-area extension package under $120,000 and save it to your shortlist
requirements.task_description: Compare two extension packages under $120,000 and select the one with the larger added floor area, then add that package to a shortlist or favorites list.

requirements.task_name: Save three highly rated loft conversion projects (4.5+ stars, completed in 2023 or later) to an Ideas list
requirements.task_description: Filter the project gallery to show loft conversions completed in or after 2023 with a rating of at least 4.5 stars, then save the first three matching projects to an Ideas or favorites list.

requirements.task_name: Generate a cost estimate for a 3-bedroom, 2-bathroom full home renovation with mid-range finishes and budget $180,000, then send the summary via on-site email form
requirements.task_description: Use the renovation cost calculator to configure a full home renovation with 3 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, mid-range finishes, and a budget of $180,000, then use the built-in email form to send the cost summary to a specified email address.

requirements.task_name: Save and reopen a 2023+ blog article about planning permits for extensions
requirements.task_description: Search the blog for articles about planning permits for extensions published in or after 2023, save the first matching article to a reading list, then reopen it from that list.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever searchBlogArticles is called with a query string that case-insensitively contains 'planning permit extension' (or its tokens) AND filters.min_year is defined and >= 2023. This should not alter the search results, only record parameters.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { query: query, min_year: filters && typeof filters.min_year === 'number' ? filters.min_year : null, max_year: filters && typeof filters.max_year === 'number' ? filters.max_year : null, category: filters && filters.category ? filters.category : null, tag: filters && filters.tag ? filters.tag : null } stored via localStorage.setItem('task6_searchParams', JSON.stringify(object)).
required_variables.reason: Tracks that the user performed a blog search using the correct topic query and a year filter of 2023 or later, enabling the evaluator to verify the search criteria rather than just the final saved article.

required_variables.set_condition: After successfully adding an article to the reading list, if the article has year >= 2023 AND its title/content/tags indicate it is about planning permits for extensions (e.g., the combined text of title, excerpt, content, and tags contains 'planning permit' and 'extension' case-insensitively). Only then record this as the candidate article for task 6.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { article_id: article.id, year: article.year || null, title: article.title || '' } stored with localStorage.setItem('task6_savedArticleMeta', JSON.stringify(object)).
required_variables.reason: Records which qualifying 2023+ 'planning permit for extensions' article was saved to the reading list so the evaluator can confirm it matches the task topic and is the basis for the reopen check.

required_variables.set_condition: When getArticleDetails(articleId) is called and localStorage.task6_savedArticleMeta exists and its article_id matches the requested articleId. This approximates that the user has reopened the same qualifying article they previously saved (likely from the reading list). Do not change the function’s return value.
required_variables.value_to_set: The articleId string passed into getArticleDetails, stored via localStorage.setItem('task6_reopenedArticleId', articleId).
required_variables.reason: Allows the evaluator to verify that the same qualifying article recorded in task6_savedArticleMeta was opened again after being saved, satisfying the 'save and reopen' requirement as closely as possible within the business logic layer.

requirements.task_name: Find the bathroom renovation warranty FAQ and send a follow-up question using the contact form
requirements.task_description: Locate the FAQ entry specifically about bathroom renovation warranty, open its details, then use the linked contact form to send a follow-up question referencing that warranty.

requirements.task_name: Create a multi-project renovation plan with a $20,000 bathroom renovation and a $25,000 deck extension
requirements.task_description: Use the multi-project quote builder to add two separate projects—a bathroom renovation with a $20,000 budget and a deck extension with a $25,000 budget—and submit the combined renovation plan.
